exercises.title: String Search Methods
exercises.description: Use find(), count(), startswith(), and endswith() to analyze a string. Print the results of each method call.
exercises.starterCode:
text = "Python programming is fun and Python is powerful"

# TODO: Print the index where 'programming' first appears (find)
print(text.find("programming"))

# TODO: Print how many times 'Python' appears (count)
print(text.count("Python"))

# TODO: Print True/False: does text start with 'Python'?
print(text.startswith("Python"))

# TODO: Print True/False: does text end with 'powerful'?
print(text.endswith("powerful"))
hints: find() returns the index of the first occurrence, or -1 if not found.
startswith() and endswith() return True or False.

exercises.title: Split and Join
exercises.description: split() breaks a string into a list of words; join() reassembles them. Use split() to get individual words, then join() them with a different separator.
exercises.starterCode:
sentence = "the quick brown fox"

# TODO: Split the sentence into words and print the list
words = sentence.split()
print(words)

# TODO: Join the words with ' | ' separator and print
joined = " | ".join(words)
print(joined)

# TODO: Print how many words are in the sentence
print(len(words))

# TODO: Split 'a,b,c,d' on ',' and print the resulting list
csv_data = "a,b,c,d"
print(csv_data.split(","))
hints: split() with no argument splits on any whitespace.

exercises.title: f-string Advanced Formatting
exercises.description: Use f-string format specs for alignment and number formatting. Create a formatted table using alignment specs.
exercises.starterCode:
# Product catalog
product1 = "Python Book"
price1 = 29.99

product2 = "Keyboard"
price2 = 89.0

product3 = "Mouse"
price3 = 15.5

# TODO: Print a header and three rows using f-string alignment
# Use :<20 for left-aligned product name (20 chars wide)
# Use :>8.2f for right-aligned price (8 chars, 2 decimal places)
print(f"{'Product':<20} {'Price':>8}")
print("-" * 30)
print(f"{product1:<20} ${price1:>7.2f}")
print(f"{product2:<20} ${price2:>7.2f}")
print(f"{product3:<20} ${price3:>7.2f}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
Product               Price
------------------------------
Python Book          $  29.99
Keyboard             $  89.00
Mouse                $  15.50
hints: :<20 means left-align in a field 20 characters wide.
:>7.2f means right-align, 7 chars wide, 2 decimal places.
The $ sign goes before the formatted number in the f-string.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: String Methods Return New Strings
warmup.description: Methods never modify the original string — they return a new one. What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
s = "  hello world  "
print(s.strip())
print(s.strip().title())
print(s.strip().replace("world", "Python"))
print(len(s.strip()))
warmup.expectedOutput:
hello world
Hello World
hello Python
11
